expectations.text: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters
expectations.evidence: Message body (before ---) is approximately 400 characters — under 1000.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label
expectations.evidence: No Voss label present. 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', 'It feels like' all absent.

expectations.text: Contains a calibrated question starting with 'how' or 'what' — not a yes/no question
expectations.evidence: 'Are you open to a quick 15-minute call this week?' is a yes/no question, not a calibrated 'how' or 'what' question.

expectations.text: Output includes the meta-line with 'Phase:' and 'Tactic:' fields
expectations.evidence: Meta-line present: 'Phase: Qualify' and 'Tactic: Answered their direct question...'

expectations.text: Meta-line declares phase as Qualify (not Label, Proof, Pull, or Call)
Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result (70%, 80%, hours-to-minutes, etc.) — too early
expectations.evidence: 'cutting manual follow-up time by 80%' and 'reducing client onboarding work by 70%' — two quantified results dropped in the Qualify phase. Too early.

expectations.text: Does not ask for a call, Zoom, or meeting
expectations.evidence: 'Are you open to a quick 15-minute call this week?' — direct call ask.
No em dashes. Plain hyphens used.

expectations.text: Reply is short/medium, not a 4-paragraph pitch (they sent one line — Aleem should not respond with a wall of text)
expectations.evidence: Four-paragraph response to James's one-line message: intro + pitch paragraph + case study paragraph + CTA paragraph. This is a wall of text.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions. All five failures are real problems: pitch dump, case studies too early, call ask before earning it, no Voss label, wall of text.
